page: UpCloud 1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the General Purpose family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU mapped to an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 25 GB local SSD for storage. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tier of cloud resources. Both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests yield a score of 742.79, reflecting the shared nature of the single vCPU. Memory bandwidth is measured at 103.88 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 7.9 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 7.92 GB/sec for large block writes. The PassMark CPU score is 2019.51, with the encryption test showing a slightly better relative performance at 1132.1.

From a resource density and cost perspective, this server represents a highly constrained hardware profile designed for minimal resource requirements. The shared CPU allocation and 1.0 GB RAM limit its capability under sustained or concurrent workloads, as demonstrated by a static web serving throughput of 1.11 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 1.31 million operations per second. It is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, basic development environments, simple cron jobs, or low-traffic static web hosting.
description: UpCloud 1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server configured with a single shared vCPU and 1.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes a bundled 25 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the lower decile, with a stress-ng score of 742.79 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 7.9 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity, this server is designed for low-demand, budget-conscious workloads. It is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, basic cron jobs, microservices, or simple static web serving.
og_description: UpCloud 1xCPU-1GB is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 25 GB SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic hosting.
meta_description: UpCloud 1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level KVM virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local SSD storage.
tagline: An entry-level, budget-friendly virtual server with shared AMD EPYC compute and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Single shared vCPU powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz
1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local SSD storage
KVM hypervisor integration with one complimentary public IPv4 address
Instruction set support including AVX, AES, and SHA_NI cryptography
Best suited for low-traffic static web serving, cron jobs, and lightweight utility tasks
